source_text: Operational effectiveness means performing similar activities better than rivals. It is necessary but not sufficient for competitive advantage. Many companies confuse operational effectiveness with strategy.
summary_text: Operational effectiveness means performing activities better than rivals. As discussed in the previous chapter on competitive advantage, this concept is necessary but not sufficient. Companies often confuse OE with strategy.
cumulative_context: - Chapter 1: Competitive advantage comes from choosing different activities, not just performing the same ones better. Porter's five forces framework helps analyze industry structure.
